(benchmark runs). See `bench/SPEC.md` for the contract.

## Parity matrix — icegres vs the Lakebase/Neon/Moonlink bar

Run: `20260705T174423Z` · binary: `icegres/target/release/icegres` · result: **18 PASS / 6 GAP / 1 N/A-BY-DESIGN** · raw: `bench/results/parity-20260705T174423Z.json`

| id | area | behavior | verdict | evidence |
|----|------|----------|---------|----------|
| A1 | wire | psql connects, simple query protocol | ✅ PASS | psql -c 'select 1' over a fresh connection returned: 1 |
| A2 | wire | extended protocol / parameterized statements | ✅ PASS | psql \bind 42 + 'where trip_id = $1' (extended query protocol) returned: 42\|Brussels |
| A3 | wire | \dt / pg_catalog introspection | ✅ PASS | \dt demo.* lists trips and cities (plus $snapshots/$manifests metadata tables): demo\|cities\|table\|postgres demo\|cities$manifests\|table\|postgres demo\|cities$snapshots\|table\|postgres demo\|trips\|table\|postgres demo\|trips$manifests\|table\|postgres demo\|trips$snapshots\|table\|postgres |
| A4 | wire | information_schema | ✅ PASS | information_schema.tables for schema demo returned: cities cities$manifests cities$snapshots trips trips$manifests trips$snapshots |
| A5 | wire | multiple concurrent connections | ✅ PASS | 8 parallel psql SELECT count(*) connections all succeeded (8/8 identical results: 285) |
| A6 | wire | server-side auth | ❌ GAP | no auth enforcement: connections with a wrong password and with nonexistent user 'not_a_real_user' both succeeded (select 1 -> 1). AuthManager::default() is a noop. |
| A7 | wire | TLS | ❌ GAP | no TLS support: psql: error: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5439 failed: server does not support SSL, but SSL was required |
| B1 | oltp | INSERT via wire, durable | ✅ PASS | INSERT trip_id=900004 returned tag 'INSERT 0 1'; row read back over a NEW connection: 900004\|Parity B1 |
| B2 | oltp | UPDATE | ❌ GAP | UPDATE rejected (iceberg-datafusion 0.9 is append-only): ERROR: UPDATE operation on table 'demo.trips' caused by This feature is not implemented: UPDATE not supported for Base table |
| B3 | oltp | DELETE | ❌ GAP | DELETE rejected (iceberg-datafusion 0.9 is append-only): ERROR: DELETE operation on table 'demo.trips' caused by This feature is not implemented: DELETE not supported for Base table |
| B4 | oltp | explicit transactions BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK | ❌ GAP | BEGIN/ROLLBACK are accepted on the wire but are non-transactional: INSERT inside BEGIN..ROLLBACK persisted (row 900005 visible after ROLLBACK, count=1). Session output: BEGIN INSERT 0 1 ROLLBACK |
| B5 | oltp | PK/constraint enforcement | ❌ GAP | no constraint enforcement: duplicate insert of trip_id=900005 accepted ('INSERT 0 1'), table now holds 2 rows with that id. Iceberg has no PK/unique constraints. |
| C1 | lakehouse | data in open format, other engines can read | ✅ PASS | independent reader pyiceberg 0.11.1 read demo.trips via the REST catalog + S3: 288 rows == server's count(288) |
| C2 | lakehouse | catalog registration (REST) | ✅ PASS | Lakekeeper GET /v1/563d3f92-7884-11f1-ab2a-cba89bbcc662/namespaces/demo/tables lists: ["cities","trips"] |
| C3 | lakehouse | CDC Postgres->Iceberg | ➖ N/A-BY-DESIGN | Moonlink exists to replicate Postgres heap data into Iceberg; icegres data is born in Iceberg (INSERT via wire commits an Iceberg snapshot directly, verified by B1+C1) — there is no second copy to synchronize. |
| C4 | lakehouse | write freshness (commit -> readable elsewhere) | ✅ PASS | row committed via conn A was readable from a new connection ~50ms after commit (coarse; includes psql startup — precise p50/p95 is bench freshness_ms). Moonlink bar: sub-second. |
| C5 | lakehouse | Iceberg metadata surfaces | ✅ PASS | demo."trips$snapshots" queryable (9 snapshots listed; $manifests also works). Caveats: 'select *' fails on the Map-typed summary column (ERROR: Unsupported Datatype Map("key_value": non-null Struct("key": non-null Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "7"}, "value": Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "8"}), unsorted)) and count(*) hits a DataFusion logical/physical schema mismatch — column projec |
| D1 | elasticity | stateless compute: restart durability | ✅ PASS | killed and restarted the :5440 compute: row count identical before/after (288), wire-inserted row trip_id=900004 intact — all state lives in Iceberg/RustFS, none in the process. |
| D2 | elasticity | multiple stateless computes on shared storage | ✅ PASS | second 'icegres serve' on :5440 (same catalog, started before the write) returned identical count (288) and served row trip_id=900004 committed via :5439 after both computes started (visible on first poll, 44ms; each query reloads table metadata from the REST catalog, so no staleness window was observed). |
| D3 | elasticity | cold start | ✅ PASS | serve spawn -> first successful 'select 1': 33ms (single run, 50ms poll; p50/p95 over >=5 runs is bench cold_start_ms). Neon bar: ~500ms-few s. |
| D4 | elasticity | time-travel read (branching/PITR analogue) | ✅ PASS | snapshot-pinned read works: demo."trips@2846737285402141775" (oldest snapshot from "trips$snapshots") returned count=280 vs current count=288 (rows appended this run are invisible in the pinned view; WHERE on the pinned table -> 280). Read-only; timestamp syntax FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF is not supported by datafusion 52. |
| D5 | elasticity | scale-to-zero | ✅ PASS | --idle-shutdown-secs 5: server answered queries (demo.cities count=20), then exited on its own 5045ms after the last connection with code 0 (clean); health pings on :5446 do not reset the idle clock. Scale-from-zero: fresh spawn answered 'select 1' after 38ms (supervisor pattern documented in icegres/src/ops.rs + README). |
| E1 | ops | structured startup logs | ✅ PASS | serve log contains structured fields catalog_uri/warehouse/s3_endpoint/listen_addr: 2026-07-05T17:44:24.535399Z INFO icegres: connecting to Iceberg REST catalog catalog_uri=http://127.0.0.1:8181/catalog warehouse=lakehouse s3_endpoint=http://127.0.0.1:9000 2026-07-05T17:44:24.555545Z INFO icegres: starting pgwire server listen_addr=127.0.0.1:5440 |
| E2 | ops | health-checkable | ✅ PASS | two probes work: (1) pgwire connect + 'select 1' as the readiness check (what the harnesses use; plain TCP connect works for tcpSocket-style checks); (2) dedicated HTTP liveness endpoint via --health-port — curl http://127.0.0.1:5446/health during the D5 probe returned 200 'ok'. |
| E3 | ops | full config via env vars | ✅ PASS | 'icegres serve' booted with ZERO flags — all config from ICEGRES_* env vars (host/port/catalog/warehouse/S3) — and answered on :5444 (demo.cities count=20) |

## Benchmark runs

<!-- bench:runs — appended by bench/bench.sh -->

### BASELINE — Bench 20260705T163255Z (accepted 2026-07-05)

**Official baseline** — `bench/results/baseline.json` (copy of
`bench/results/bench-20260705T163255Z.json`); parity twin:
`bench/results/parity-baseline.json` (run `20260705T163411Z`,
**16 PASS / 8 GAP / 1 N/A-BY-DESIGN**).

Binary profile: `icegres/target/release/icegres` (**release**, pinned matrix
iceberg 0.9.1 / datafusion 52.5.0 / datafusion-postgres 0.15.0 / arrow 57.3.1).
Harness: 3 warmups discarded, 20 iterations per metric, 5 cold-start runs,
single 10 s qps window.

Acceptance per SPEC §2: two consecutive full runs (`20260705T163201Z` →
`20260705T163255Z`) agree within 25% on **every** metric — worst p50 delta
9.06% (filtered_scan_ms); all deltas: aggregate +8.61%, filtered_scan +9.06%,
join +7.55%, point_lookup +3.64%, connect 0%, cold_start +0.24%,
insert_single −5.1%, insert_batch100 −5.38%, freshness −6.77%, qps −7.32%,
rss_idle −1.65%, binary_size 0%.

Table state frozen for reads: `demo.trips` = 7,338 rows (280 seeded + rows
appended by earlier harness-verification runs; append-only Iceberg, cannot
shrink). Write metrics (insert_single/insert_batch100/freshness) target the
bench-owned `demo.bench_scratch` table, created fresh and dropped by
`bench/bench.sh` each run, so `demo.trips` no longer grows during benchmarks.

| metric | p50 | p95 | n / detail |
|--------|-----|-----|------------|
| connect_ms | 0.2 | 0.24 | n=20 |
| point_lookup_ms | 221.64 | 227.2 | n=20 |
| filtered_scan_ms | 230.12 | 238.83 | n=20 |
| aggregate_ms | 253.96 | 276.14 | n=20 |
| join_ms | 257.8 | 282.92 | n=20 |
| insert_single_ms | 47.66 | 60.8 | n=20 |
| insert_batch100_ms | 82.68 | 96.4 | n=20 |
| freshness_ms | 61.99 | 71.66 | n=20 |
| qps_8conn | 7.6 | — | 8 conns, 10s window |
| cold_start_ms | 45.4 | 45.43 | n=5 |
| binary_size_mb | 150.88 | — | |
| rss_idle_mb | 81.65 | — | |

#### Run history (raw JSONs in `bench/results/`)

- `20260705T162147Z` — harness v1 first full run (near-fresh table; reads ~21 ms,
  qps 82). Superseded: harness v1 wrote to `demo.trips`.
- `20260705T162511Z`, `20260705T162606Z` — baseline-stability attempt with
  harness v1: consecutive runs drifted 40–82% because each run appended
  ~2,500 rows / ~46 Parquet files to `demo.trips`. Discarded; harness fixed to
  write to `demo.bench_scratch` instead.
- `20260705T163201Z` — agreement partner for the accepted baseline (fixed
  harness, run 1 of 2).
- `20260705T163255Z` — first accepted baseline (fixed harness, run 2 of 2);
  superseded by `20260705T164622Z` (metadata-caching improvement).
- `20260705T164622Z` — metadata-caching improvement accepted through the
  gate; superseded by `20260705T171214Z` (datafusion-tuning improvement).
- `20260705T171214Z` — datafusion-tuning improvement accepted through the
  gate; superseded by `20260705T172127Z` (seed-file-layout improvement).
- `20260705T172127Z` — **BASELINE** (seed-file-layout improvement accepted
  through the gate; see Improvement history below). NOTE: demo tables were
  dropped (Lakekeeper REST, `purgeRequested=true`) and re-seeded as one
  Parquet file per table before this run, so read metrics are not
  layout-comparable with earlier runs — that layout change IS the
  improvement.

## Improvement history

### 2026-07-05 — metadata-caching (accepted; baseline → `bench-20260705T164622Z.json`)

**Change** (`icegres/src/cache.rs`, wired in `icegres/src/context.rs`):
snapshot-aware table metadata caching. iceberg-datafusion's catalog-backed
provider called `load_table` on every scan and rebuilt a `Table` whose
internal manifest cache starts cold, so every query re-read the manifest
list + all ~231 manifest avro files from RustFS (~1 ms/GET ≈ the entire
~220 ms point-lookup p50). `CachingTableProvider` now keeps the last-seen
`Table` (warm moka manifest/manifest-list cache) inside an
`IcebergStaticTableProvider` and performs one cheap REST `load_table`
(~2–3 ms against local Lakekeeper) per scan purely to detect metadata
changes: unchanged → reuse warm provider; changed (commit from this server
or any other writer) → rebuild from fresh metadata. **Freshness is exact —
every scan observes the catalog's current snapshot; no TTL, no staleness
window.** INSERTs delegate unchanged to the upstream catalog-backed
provider; metadata tables (`$snapshots`/`$manifests`) and DDL fall through
to the upstream schema provider.

**Gate PASS** vs baseline `20260705T163255Z` (e2e green, 19 assertions;
parity rerun `20260705T164753Z`: 16 PASS / 8 GAP / 1 N/A — zero verdict
changes):

| metric (p50) | baseline | new | delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| point_lookup_ms | 221.64 | 14.74 | **−93.3%** |
| filtered_scan_ms | 230.12 | 14.95 | **−93.5%** |
| aggregate_ms | 253.96 | 121.56 | **−52.1%** |
| join_ms | 257.80 | 127.39 | **−50.6%** |
| qps_8conn | 7.6 | 28.1 | **+269.7%** |
| connect_ms | 0.20 | 0.21 | +5% |
| insert_single_ms | 47.66 | 53.58 | +12.4% (within gate/noise) |
| insert_batch100_ms | 82.68 | 90.74 | +9.7% (within gate/noise) |
| freshness_ms | 61.99 | 69.95 | +12.8% (within gate/noise) |
| cold_start_ms | 45.40 | 45.40 | 0% |

Remaining read cost is data-file GETs + Parquet footer reads (231 small
files in `demo.trips` — compaction is SPEC §4.4) plus the per-scan
`load_table` freshness check.

### 2026-07-05 — datafusion-tuning (accepted; baseline → `bench-20260705T171214Z.json`)

**Measurement first** (interleaved A/B sweeps on :5444, 20 iters/query +
5–8 s qps windows, upstream controls repeated): every classic
SessionConfig/RuntimeEnv knob was a no-op beyond run noise on this
workload — `target_partitions` 1/2/4/8/16, `batch_size` 1024/8192,
`coalesce_batches` on/off, `repartition_aggregations/joins` on/off.
`EXPLAIN ANALYZE` shows why: all DataFusion operators complete in µs;
~all latency is `fetch_time` waiting on the single-partition
`IcebergTableScan` stream, whose manifest/data-file IO against RustFS is
hardwired to `num_cpus` (= 4) concurrent GETs in iceberg-rust 0.9. The
effective "object-store connection/concurrency" knob for RustFS therefore
lives in the Iceberg scan builder, not in `SessionConfig`.

**Change**:
- `icegres/src/scan.rs` (new): `TunedIcebergScan` — drop-in
  `ExecutionPlan` built from the upstream `IcebergTableScan`'s public
  getters (table/snapshot/projection/predicate/limit); runs the *same*
  `TableScan` with `.with_concurrency_limit(N)` so manifest + data-file
  GETs overlap. `N` from `ICEGRES_SCAN_CONCURRENCY` (default 32, tuned:
  8/16/24/32/64 swept, 16–32 equivalent, 64 hurt qps; `0` = upstream
  behavior). Wired in `CachingTableProvider::scan` (cache.rs); non-scan
  plans (metadata tables, inserts, DDL) pass through untouched.
- `icegres/src/context.rs`: `ICEGRES_DF_OPTS` env passthrough
  (`;`-separated `datafusion.*=value` pairs) applied to `SessionConfig` at
  startup — the mechanism used for the sweeps, kept for ops tuning;
  defaults deliberately left at DataFusion's since no setting measured an
  improvement.

**Gate PASS** vs baseline `20260705T164622Z` (e2e green; parity rerun
`20260705T171352Z`: 16 PASS / 8 GAP / 1 N/A — zero verdict changes):

| metric (p50) | baseline | new | delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| point_lookup_ms | 14.74 | 11.68 | **−20.8%** |
| filtered_scan_ms | 14.95 | 12.26 | **−18.0%** |
| aggregate_ms | 121.56 | 90.18 | **−25.8%** |
| join_ms | 127.39 | 99.64 | **−21.8%** |
| freshness_ms | 69.95 | 59.92 | **−14.3%** |
| connect_ms | 0.21 | 0.19 | −9.5% |
| qps_8conn | 28.1 | 28.1 | 0% (8 conns already saturate 4 cores) |
| insert_single_ms | 53.58 | 52.44 | −2.1% |
| insert_batch100_ms | 90.74 | 91.68 | +1.0% (noise) |
| cold_start_ms | 45.40 | 45.04 | −0.8% |

Remaining read cost: per-file Parquet open/footer+data GETs across ~236
tiny files even at IO concurrency 32 — file-count, not knob, bound;
compaction/seed-layout is SPEC §4.4 and is the next lever.

### 2026-07-05 — seed-file-layout (accepted; baseline → `bench-20260705T172127Z.json`)

**Change** (`icegres/src/seed.rs`, `icegres/src/context.rs`): seed now
writes each demo table as exactly ONE Parquet file in ONE Iceberg commit.
`insert_chunked` (100-row chunks → 3 commits for trips) became
`insert_all` (single 280-row `INSERT`), and the seed session pins
`target_partitions = 1` via the new
`context::build_session_context_with(catalog, Some(1))` —
iceberg-datafusion round-robin-repartitions unpartitioned-table INSERT
input across `target_partitions` workers and writes one file per
non-empty worker, so 1 partition ⇒ 1 data file deterministically. The
`serve`/`sql` paths are unchanged (`build_session_context` delegates with
`None`).

**No `icegres compact` subcommand — documented why not** (SPEC §4.4 made
it conditional): the pinned iceberg-rust 0.9.1 `Transaction` API exposes
only `fast_append` plus property/sort-order/statistics/location updates
(`~/.cargo/.../iceberg-0.9.1/src/transaction/`) — there is **no
rewrite-files/replace-files/overwrite action**, so rewriting N small
files into one cannot remove the source files atomically without
hand-forging a REST commit outside the library's conflict detection.
Recovery path instead: drop the demo tables via Lakekeeper REST
(`purgeRequested=true`) and re-run `icegres seed` (done for this run:
`demo.trips` went from 239 data files / dozens of snapshots to 1 file /
1 snapshot; `demo.cities` 1 file).

**Gate PASS** vs baseline `20260705T171214Z` (e2e green, 19 assertions;
parity rerun `20260705T172254Z`: 16 PASS / 8 GAP / 1 N/A — zero verdict
changes, parity gate "no parity downgrades"):

| metric (p50) | baseline | new | delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| point_lookup_ms | 11.68 | 6.36 | **−45.5%** |
| filtered_scan_ms | 12.26 | 5.88 | **−52.0%** |
| aggregate_ms | 90.18 | 6.39 | **−92.9%** |
| join_ms | 99.64 | 9.46 | **−90.5%** |
| qps_8conn | 28.1 | 459.1 | **+1533.8%** |
| insert_single_ms | 52.44 | 49.27 | −6.0% |
| insert_batch100_ms | 91.68 | 83.42 | −9.0% |
| freshness_ms | 59.92 | 60.07 | +0.3% (noise) |
| cold_start_ms | 45.04 | 44.93 | −0.2% |
| connect_ms | 0.19 | 0.22 | +15.8% (sub-ms noise, within gate) |
| rss_idle_mb | 86.23 | 76.93 | −10.8% |

This confirms the datafusion-tuning diagnosis: reads were file-count
bound. With 1–2 data files per table, every read metric is now
5–10 ms-class and throughput is catalog-freshness-check bound (per-scan
REST `load_table`), the next candidate lever. Note: e2e keeps appending
1 row (= 1 new small file) per run to `demo.trips`; layout stays
near-optimal until many runs accumulate, and the reset path above
restores it.

### 2026-07-05 — parity-features (accepted; baseline → `bench-20260705T173613Z.json`, parity baseline → `parity-20260705T174423Z.json`)

**Change** — closed the two cheap parity GAPs and hardened E2 (SPEC §4.5):

- **D5 scale-to-zero** (`icegres/src/ops.rs`, wired in `main.rs`):
  `icegres serve --idle-shutdown-secs N` (env `ICEGRES_IDLE_SHUTDOWN_SECS`)
  exits cleanly (code 0) after N consecutive seconds with zero client
  connections (countdown also starts at boot). Implemented as our own
  accept loop over the re-exported `pgwire::tokio::process_socket` with the
  same `DfSessionService` + no-op auth as upstream's private
  `HandlerFactory`; an atomic connection counter + idle-since clock feed a
  250 ms watchdog. **The flagless path still uses the stock
  datafusion-postgres `serve()` loop, byte-for-byte unchanged.** Supervisor
  pattern (systemd / `while :; do … done` respawn loop) documented in
  ops.rs module docs + README. Parity probe now starts a compute with
  `--idle-shutdown-secs 5`, queries it, watches it exit on its own (5045 ms
  after the last connection, exit code 0), then respawns it and gets
  `select 1` back in ~1.3 s → **D5 GAP → PASS**.
- **D4 time travel** (`icegres/src/cache.rs`): latent support surfaced —
  `IcebergStaticTableProvider::try_new_from_table_snapshot` existed unused
  in iceberg-datafusion 0.9.1. `CachingSchemaProvider` now resolves quoted
  `"table@snapshot_id"` references to a read-only provider pinned to that
  snapshot (immutable → cached forever; unknown ids fail with 'snapshot id
  … not found'; INSERT into pinned tables rejected). Probe pins the oldest
  snapshot from `"trips$snapshots"`: count=280 vs current 288, rows
  appended this run invisible in the pinned view → **D4 GAP → PASS**.
- **E2 health** (`icegres/src/ops.rs`): `--health-port P` (env
  `ICEGRES_HEALTH_PORT`) serves a minimal HTTP liveness endpoint (any
  request → `200 OK`, body `ok`); TCP-connect checks documented in README.
  Health traffic deliberately does NOT reset the idle clock. E2 stays PASS
  with strictly better evidence (dedicated endpoint verified by curl).

**Parity: 16 PASS / 8 GAP / 1 N/A → 18 PASS / 6 GAP / 1 N/A** (run
`20260705T174423Z`), zero downgrades. e2e green (19 assertions).

**Gate + the layout-drift finding.** The first gate run vs
`baseline.json` (= `bench-20260705T172127Z`, captured at 280 rows / 1 data
file right after the seed-file-layout re-seed) FAILed on qps_8conn (−15 to
−24%) and aggregate (+28%). A/B isolation proved this is **environmental,
not the change**: a control binary with the parity-features edits fully
removed (prior kept improvements intact) showed the *same* failures vs
that baseline (`bench-20260705T174156Z`: qps −23.5%, aggregate +28.2%) —
demo.trips had drifted to 284 rows / 5 data files from the 4 rows the
previous session's own e2e+parity runs appended after its bench (append-only
by design; re-seeding was not permitted in this session). Candidate vs
control **at matched layout** is a clean PASS, nothing regresses:

| metric (p50) | control (old code) | candidate | delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| point_lookup_ms | 6.68 | 6.62 | −0.9% |
| filtered_scan_ms | 6.41 | 5.83 | −9.0% |
| aggregate_ms | 8.19 | 7.09 | −13.4% |
| join_ms | 10.59 | 9.77 | −7.7% |
| connect_ms | 0.24 | 0.24 | 0% |
| insert_single_ms | 49.28 | 55.20 | +12% (noise, within gate) |
| insert_batch100_ms | 88.30 | 91.74 | +3.9% |
| freshness_ms | 59.02 | 62.60 | +6.1% |
| cold_start_ms | 45.37 | 45.49 | +0.3% |
| qps_8conn | 351.1 | 391.5 | +11.5% |

Two candidate runs (`173613Z`, `173715Z`) agree within 25% on every metric
(SPEC §2 noise rule); the first becomes the new `baseline.json`. NOTE for
future gates: every e2e/parity run appends rows to demo.trips (~1 ms/extra
data file on every scan; qps is the most sensitive), so a candidate
measured N runs after the baseline carries N×~4 files of drift — if the
gate fails on qps/aggregate only, benchmark a control build at the same
layout before blaming the change.
